commit.message:
Fix probability distribution collapse, tooltip CSS visibility, and conclusion phrasing (#91)

* Initial plan

* Fix probability distribution compression, tooltip CSS, and conclusion language

- backend/intelligence/evented_pipeline.py:
  - Add _LIE_SIM_AMPLIFICATION=4 constant: raises lie_sim to power 4 before
    computing Bayesian posterior, spreading [0.79,1.0] cosine cluster from
    compressed ~52%/45% to differentiated ~54%/20%/20% distributions
  - Replace _ENTROPY_COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD with _COLLAPSE_TOP_PROB_FLOOR and
    _COLLAPSE_GAP_FLOOR for more intuitive collapse detection
  - Fix inverted distribution-collapse warning logic (collapse = uniform = low gap)
  - Fix dual_results pairing: match by (pattern_a, pattern_b, pattern_c) key
    instead of array index to avoid wrong tooltip data on mismatched transitions
  - Update tooltip_data.bayesian to include lie_sim_amplified, amplification
    fields and mention the ^k formula in the calculation string
  - Update tooltip_data.lie_algebra note to explain the amplification
  - Add dual_integration nonlinear emergence dims to tooltip_data
  - Change _evidence_summary_raw from "Primary projected outcome: ..." to
    "Likely outcome (XX%): ..." for analyst-readable language
  - Remove max(posterior, 1e-4) floor in composition path; inverse path keeps
    the small floor so low-probability high-impact paths remain visible

- frontend/app.py:
  - Add CSS overrides for Streamlit container overflow:visible so tooltip box
    is not clipped by parent layout containers
  - Change tooltip position from bottom: calc(100%+8px) to top: calc(100%+6px)
    (show below, not above) to avoid top-overflow clipping
  - Expand _prob_tooltip_html to show lie_sim_amplified, note, dual nonlinear
    emergence dims, consistency score, and verdict in tooltip
  - Add hoverable probability badge to Conclusion tab using top probability
    tree node so users can inspect the math from the Conclusion tab directly

- tests/test_evented_pipeline.py:
  - Import new constants _LIE_SIM_AMPLIFICATION, _COLLAPSE_TOP_PROB_FLOOR,
    _COLLAPSE_GAP_FLOOR
  - Add TestProbabilityDistributionSpread: 3 tests verifying top probability
    > floor and top1-top2 gap > floor (detects distribution collapse)
  - Add TestTooltipDataFields: 7 tests verifying all required tooltip_data
    fields present (bayesian, lie_algebra, dual_integration, patterns) and
    formula mentions amplification and lie_sim_amplified is consistent

- tests/test_pipeline_schema_contract.py:
  - Update test_evidence_path_summary_outcome_first to accept both
    "Likely outcome" and "Primary projected outcome" prefixes

- backend/intelligence/evented_pipeline.py:
  - Improve _bayes_calc string to show intermediate lie_sim_amplified value in

Fix remote session state: use full content + shared detector

Remote sessions showed 'waiting' even when active because:

1. ops.list_sessions_full() only read 3 lines (tail_lines=3) for the
   /api/sessions endpoint. With only 3 lines of prompt text, the
   detector always classified sessions as 'waiting'. Fixed: now reads
   50 lines (same as local detection) and returns 5 preview lines.

2. mcp_http.py /api/sessions created a fresh PaneDetector(ZpilotConfig())
   on each request — a default-config detector with no state history.
   Fixed: uses the app-scoped _detector which has proper config and
   accumulates detection history across polls for accurate state.

Also added 3 new MCP tools: node_info, check_auth, repair_auth.

commit.message:
fix(maglev): count 1-arg and 2-arg call sites for R15 initialization

The mono_call_sites counter only counted 0-arg calls, but
emit_direct_call_1/2 also use R15 for finish_direct_call_r15.
This caused null pointer dereference on Linux CI when a function
had only 1-arg or 2-arg calls (e.g. add(10,20) in the tiering test)
because R15 was never initialized in the prologue.

Fix: count calls with 0-2 args as mono call sites so R15 is
initialized and mono call cache slots are allocated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sprint-9): R-04 Dante auto-reconnect, R-11 SCHED_FIFO RT, R-12 DSP alloc notes, R-13 ETag locking, S-04 TLS via rustls

R-04: Dante device task wrapped in exponential-backoff retry loop (2s→60s).
      Shutdown signal cancels both the retry and the delay.
R-11: try_set_rt_priority_once() sets SCHED_FIFO pri 90 on first DSP callback
      invocation (Linux only, thread-local flag, needs CAP_SYS_NICE or rtprio limit).
R-12: Audit comments on hot-path allocs in audio callback; future D-01 work.
R-13: state_version AtomicU64 in AppState; bump_version()/etag() helpers.
      GET /api/v1/state returns ETag header (W/"N").
      PATCH /api/v1/matrix/:in/:out checks If-Match and returns 412 on mismatch.
      All other mutation handlers (mute/solo/name/gain/load-scene) bump version.
S-04: axum-server + rustls optional feature 'tls'. --tls-cert/--tls-key CLI args
      (PATCHBOX_TLS_CERT/PATCHBOX_TLS_KEY env vars). Build with --features tls
      to enable HTTPS. Without the flag, falls back to plain HTTP.

commit.message:
fix: restore `override` lock-file handling after launcher refactor (#4532)

* fix: update test to assert warnings for invalid CLI arguments

* chore: adding changelog file 4519.fixed.md [dependabot-skip]

* feat: add log printing step to test-remote workflow for better debugging

* fix: clear MAPDL database before resume() to prevent segfault in LoadElements

MAPDL was crashing with a Segmentation Violation in LoadElements during
CI. The crash was triggered by the _save_selection context manager
(which creates temporary __CMP_*__ components and runs NSLE/LoadElements)
encountering a stale or inconsistent database state left over from a
previous test.

Root cause: function-scoped resume() fixtures in test_post.py, and
class-scoped setup fixtures in test_component.py and
test_component_queries.py, were loading or creating database state
without first clearing any leftover state from prior tests.

Changes:
- tests/test_post.py: add clear(mapdl) before mapdl.resume() in all
  four resume fixtures (Test_static_solve, Test_plastic_solve,
  Test_contact_solve, Test_thermal_solve); remove redundant prep7()/
  solution() calls superseded by clear(); make Test_thermal_solve
  inherit TestClass for consistent class-level cleanup; import clear
  from conftest
- tests/test_component.py: add clear(mapdl) at the start of
  Test_components.setup to make the cleanup explicit and remove
  the implicit ordering dependency on TestClass.initializer; import
  clear from conftest
- tests/test_inline_functions/test_component_queries.py: add
  clear(mapdl) at the start of solved_box and twisted_sheet class-
  scoped fixtures; import clear from conftest
- docker/testing/Dockerfile: enable PYMAPDL_DEBUG_TESTING for better
  CI diagnostics
- docker/testing/docker-compose.yml: comment out custom entrypoint/
  command to restore default MAPDL startup behaviour

* fix: restore override lock-file handling after launcher refactor

The check_lock_file() logic was not ported when launcher.py was split
into modules in PR #4416.  As a result, override=True had no effect and
MAPDL would fail to start whenever a stale .lock file existed in the
run_location directory.

- validation.py: add _validate_lock_file(); raises an error when a lock
  file is present and override=False, or a warning when override=True
  (file will be removed before launch).  Called from validate_config().
- process.py: add _handle_lock_file(); deletes the lock file when
  override=True, raises LockFileException when override=False (safety
  net in case validation was bypassed).  Called at the top of
  launch_mapdl_process().
- tests: 5 new tests in TestValidateLockFile, 6 in TestHandleLockFile.

Fixes #4526

commit.message:
feat(ws): structure-aware repo matching, red errors, --all flag exclusivity

Three targeting improvements:

1. Structure-aware --repo-name matching
   For 'list' structure workspaces, --repo-name now accepts the last
   slash-delimited segment of a repo's config name (with .git stripped),
   so '-r api' matches 'org/team/api' and 'org/team/api.git'.
   Exact full-name match is still accepted for both structures.
   - internal/workspace/targeting.go: new repoNameMatches() helper
   - internal/workspace/exec.go: all 3 filter checks use repoNameMatches
   - internal/workspace/workspace.go: all 6 filter checks use repoNameMatches
   - internal/workspace/targeting_test.go: 4 new list-structure tests

2. Red error messages
   All errors returned from subcommand RunE are now printed in red to
   stderr. Previously cobra printed them as plain 'Error: <msg>' text.
   - zgard/root.go: SilenceErrors/SilenceUsage set to true; Execute()
     prints err via clr.Red to stderr before os.Exit(1)

3. --all mutual exclusivity with --project-name / --repo-name
   Using --all together with -p/--project-name or -r/--repo-name is
   now a hard error — the flags are logically incompatible since --all
   operates across every workspace, while project/repo filters scope
   to a specific workspace's structure.
   - zgard/ws/ws.go: two new checks in PersistentPreRunE

commit.message:
Fix for-in enumeration on Proxy objects

When a Proxy object was encountered in the GetKeys opcode (used by for-in),
the code was reading the proxy's internal map keys (__proxy_target__, etc.)
instead of using the proxy's [[OwnPropertyKeys]] trap. This caused for-in
to produce no keys for Proxy objects.

Fix: detect Proxy VmObjects in GetKeys and use Reflect.ownKeys +
Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor to collect enumerable string keys,
then walk the target's prototype chain.

Fixes 5 test262 tests in built-ins/Proxy (cluster 35).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add EntityMoveBatch (0x0107) and EntityMoveCompact (0x0108) wire types

Batched entity move format: single MsgHeader+server_tick per UDP segment
instead of per-entity headers. EntityMoveCompact is 32 bytes (no header,
no server_tick) vs old 42-byte EntityMove with redundant per-entity header.

- 35 entities/segment vs 27 = 21% fewer segments per player
- 32B = single AVX2 vmovdqu register (was 2 loads + 2 stores for 42B)
- Also adds missing hp/stamina fields to StateAck schema

Benchmarked at 500 players: tick -12%, send -41% vs prior format.

commit.message:
fix: rewrite revert to use direct functions, not fragile patch file

Root cause: revert_all_changes depended on a .edog-changes.patch file
generated by apply_all_changes. Three ways this failed:

1. Token refresh called apply_all_changes again, which overwrote the
   patch file. If any revert function returned unchanged content (e.g.
   regex mismatch), that file was silently dropped from the new patch.

2. Double Ctrl+C during shutdown: stop_flt_service waits up to 10s,
   user presses Ctrl+C again, second KeyboardInterrupt kills the
   handler before revert_all_changes runs.

3. apply_patch_reverse had a false positive 'already reverted' path
   that deleted the patch when git said 'patch does not apply' — even
   if files were still dirty.

Fix: rewrite revert_all_changes to call each revert function directly
on the file content. No patch file dependency. Each file is reverted
independently with its own try/except. Also: block SIGINT during
cleanup to prevent double Ctrl+C from skipping revert.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][sh] Protect network-manager from autoremove cascade

network-manager is the networking daemon on Pi OS Bookworm, installed
by pi-gen stage2. Without apt-mark manual, autoremove sweeps it after
dev packages are purged, leaving the image with only the lo interface.

Also protect firmware-brcm80211 (WiFi firmware), raspberrypi-net-mods,
and net-tools.

* [sh] add linting check for quarter-block Unicode characters to prevent rendering issues on Raspberry Pi

* [sh] check-quarter-blocks.sh: improve comments and maintainability

* [ai][sh] Fix cleanup cascade removing runtime packages

Three cascade paths were destroying packages needed at runtime:

1. purge libbluetooth3 → cascade network-manager (networking daemon)
2. purge triggerhappy  → cascade raspi-config → orphan raspberrypi-sys-mods
3. purge xkb-data      → cascade console-setup, keyboard-configuration

Additionally, pi-gen export-image/02-set-sources runs
  apt-get -y dist-upgrade --auto-remove --purge
AFTER our stage, sweeping any remaining auto-installed orphans.

Fixes:
- Move apt-mark manual to section 0, before any purge operations
- Remove libbluetooth3 from purge (network-manager hard dep)
- Remove triggerhappy from purge (raspi-config hard dep)
- Remove xkb-data from purge (console-setup hard dep)
- Add libbluetooth3, triggerhappy, alsa-utils, xkb-data, parted
  to the apt-mark manual list
- Patch export-image dist-upgrade to drop --auto-remove flag

This restores raspberrypi-sys-mods (firstboot), network-manager
(eth0), raspi-config, console-setup, and keyboard-configuration
to the final image.

apt-mark manual does not protect against cascade from apt-get purge -y.
After three attempts to prevent the cascade by removing trigger packages,
network-manager is still being removed by an unidentified indirect path.

Add a verification step after autoremove that checks whether critical
runtime packages (network-manager, raspberrypi-sys-mods, raspi-config)
survived the purge. If any are missing, reinstall them before cleaning
the apt cache.

Also add libnm0 and libndp0 (NM core libraries) to the apt-mark manual
protection list.

* [ai][sh] Fix MOTD warning, TLS directory permissions, and PDF audit diagnostics

Three issues found during first-boot audit (make dev-ssh-audit):

1. MOTD warning not shown: velocity user had no NOPASSWD sudoers
   entry, so sudo -n getent shadow failed silently. The fail-safe
   was inverted — it showed the welcome banner instead of the
   warning. Fix: add /etc/sudoers.d/020_velocity-nopasswd in the
   image build, and flip the fail-safe so failed checks show the
   warning (fail towards caution).

2. TLS certs reported missing: the generate-tls script created
   the TLS directory with umask 077 (mode 700, root-owned). Certs
   were present and nginx was serving them, but the audit SSH
   (running as velocity) could not access the directory. Fix: set
   directory to 755; individual file permissions (600 for keys,
   644 for certs) provide the actual protection.

3. PDF generation HTTP 500 with no details: the audit now captures
   the last few journal entries from the service log when PDF
   generation fails, so the actual Python error is visible.

* [ai][sh][py] Fix map not included in PDF — librsvg2-bin removed by cascade

The SVG-to-PDF conversion for map inclusion silently failed because
librsvg2-bin (rsvg-convert) was removed by the X11 purge cascade in
section 9 of the cleanup script.  librsvg2-bin depends on libcairo2,
which depends on libx11-6, so purging X11 libraries cascades through
to the SVG converter.

Fix: add librsvg2-bin to the critical package reinstall list in
section 13, alongside network-manager and raspi-config.

Also add a diagnostic print to SVGToPDFConverter.convert() so that
when all three converters (cairosvg, inkscape, rsvg-convert) fail,
the failure is reported instead of swallowed silently.

perf(maglev): enable global promotion for functions with user calls

Previously, codegen-level global promotion was completely disabled when
any Call/Construct node existed anywhere in the function. This meant
benchmarks like prototype_chain, sieve_primes, and closure_counter
(which have calls in their setup code) lost all global promotion
benefits in their hot loops.

Now the codegen emits flush (sta_global) before and reload (lda_global)
after each user call node, keeping promoted register-file copies in
sync with GlobalEnv. The flush saves/restores live caller-saved
registers; the reload saves/restores all caller-saved registers
unconditionally since post-call liveness is not easily available.

For benchmarks with calls only in setup (prototype_chain, sieve_primes),
the flush/reload cost is paid once while the hot loop runs at full
speed with promoted globals in R14 slots.

EXP-1161-1170: Causal PK analysis — leakage confirmed at 100%

DEFINITIVE FINDING: PK temporal lead improvement is 100% leakage.
Causal projection adds exactly zero at all lead times (15-75 min).
The model already extracts PK decay from the window; the leaked
lead provides future bolus decisions not available at prediction time.

Causal alternatives discovered:
- PK momentum: +0.010, 10/11 wins (best causal PK feature)
- PK trajectory: +0.008, 8/11 wins
- PK rate of change: +0.006, 7/11 wins
- Basal-only lead: +0.059 (semi-causal for known schedules)

Validated leakage-free SOTA remains R²=0.581 (EXP-1128).
Causal SOTA pipeline: R²=0.531 (EXP-1170).

- Changed INNER JOIN to LEFT JOIN in kiosk_extract_print_items to handle deleted food items
- Added null check for billTotal element in renderBill
- Added scan response items logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement master proxy strategy with server-side Piped fallback

Backend Improvements:
- Add dual-layer extraction: play-dl → Piped API fallback
- Server-side Piped API call eliminates browser CORS issues
- Return source indicator (play-dl or piped) in response
- Better error logging for debugging extraction failures
- 15s timeout for more reliable extraction attempts

Frontend Improvements:
- Remove client-side Piped iframe fallback (now handled server-side)
- Add error state display with user-friendly messages
- Add source badge showing extraction method (Direct/Piped)
- Improved loading UI with "Trying multiple sources" message
- Cleaner error handling and user feedback

Why This Works:
✓ Zero CORS errors - browser only talks to Render backend
✓ Double-layer reliability - play-dl fails → Piped API kicks in
✓ Simplified frontend - single API endpoint, no fallback logic needed
✓ Better UX - users see which source is being used

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add score clamping to ALL API endpoints

Defense in depth - clamp scores at every layer:
- app.py: _clamp_response() wraps /step endpoints
- server/app.py: Same treatment for all endpoints
- All /grader, /baseline, /rl/* endpoints now clamp
- Match scores in observation lists also clamped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
